businessBackground: Magnera Corporation is the world's largest nonwovens company, formed November 4, 2024 via a Reverse Morris Trust transaction combining Berry Global Group's Health, Hygiene & Specialties (HHS) division with Glatfelter Corporation. Pre-Transaction Berry (Treasure) shareholders received 90% of the combined company; GLT shareholders retained 10%. Berry (Treasure) was the accounting acquirer.

cyclical.context: China softening but not Magnera's primary market; tangential signal
cyclical.name: China PP futures (CNY/T)

cyclical.context: Mfg-side strength supports European nonwovens demand; mixed signal vs services contraction
cyclical.current: 52.2 in April 2026 (8-month high; Mar 51.6); Eurozone composite 48.6 (services dragging)
cyclical.name: Eurozone Manufacturing PMI

cyclical.context: Tangential — automotive nonwovens secondary end-market for MAGN; more directly material for TRI-LSE peer-context
cyclical.current: Q1 2026 +4% YoY (ACEA, March surge + EV incentives); BEV share 19.4%. Reverses Feb YTD -1.2%
cyclical.name: EU auto registrations (% YoY)

cyclical.context: Forced-selling-from-BERY-divestiture overhang resolved; sympathetic-reading-through catalyst removed (neutral)
cyclical.current: BERY-Amcor merger complete; no MAGN-specific repatriation commentary in past 7 days
cyclical.name: Berry Global (BERY) parent activity

noiseFilter: **Wells Fargo PT changes without model revision** — PT path $22→$14→$12 over ~6 months without public Q1 FY2026 model rebuild. Per Rule §17, treat as market-state indicator only. Do NOT use as FV anchor or thesis input.
**Daily MAGN price moves on thin volume (~244K-372K shares vs 352K 20-day avg)** ahead of scheduled Q2 7 May earnings. Float behaviour, not thesis signal.
**CR Report (Apr 22) ESG credentialing** in isolation — no financial catalyst. Adjacent to V3 institutional discovery pathway but does NOT trigger thesis revision on its own.
**Berry Global (BERY) quarterly result reads** when no MAGN-specific commentary — passive cross-reference, not signal.
**Director RSU grants (e.g., Mary Dean Hall 12,998 RSU 10 Mar 2026)** — non-cash compensation, not insider conviction signal.
**Routine supply-chain reshuffling commentary on tariffs** — Magnera management views direct tariff cost as limited; only escalation to confirmed margin compression matters.

structural.current: ~$0.62/lb (March/April 2026), **+34% YoY**. PDH unit outages sustaining feedstock pressure.
structural.name: **Polypropylene cost (US PGP spot, $/lb)**
structural.source: BusinessAnalytiq Apr 2026 [^26]; Plastics Today Apr 2026 [^28]; ResinSmart [^27]
structural.threshold: Sustained >$0.65/lb for 2 consecutive quarters with Americas EBITDA margin compression >100bps = structural cost-pass-through gap

structural.name: **EU April propylene contract (MCP, €/t)**
structural.threshold: Sustained >€1,400/t for 2 consecutive quarters with RoW EBITDA margin <8% = European cost-pass-through gap

structural.current: 13.1% FY2025; 13.2% Q1 FY2026. Magnera's strongest region.
structural.name: **Americas Adj EBITDA margin (%)**
structural.source: MAGN 10-K p.28 + Q1 FY2026 10-Q p.8; brief reframes as Q2 7 May watch-item
structural.threshold: <12% for 2 consecutive quarters = cost-shock structural; <10% = bear-case validation

structural.current: 8.2% FY2025; 9.9% Q1 FY2026. Improving but still below Americas.
structural.name: **RoW Adj EBITDA margin (%)**
structural.threshold: <7% for 2 consecutive quarters = European softness structural; >12% = synergy delivery validated

structural.current: 3.8x at Q1 FY2026; guided 3.0x by FY-end. Net debt $1.65B / EBITDA $362M (FY2025). $264M cash; $550M available liquidity.
structural.name: **Leverage (Net Debt / EBITDA)**
structural.source: MAGN 10-K + Q1 FY2026 10-Q; FY2026 guide $380-410M EBITDA reaffirmed

structural.current: Mid-teens realised FY2025; $25M FY2026 target (of $55M total program). Q1 FY2026 "tracking" per management; no precise quarterly $ disclosed.
structural.name: **Synergy capture pace (cumulative $M, FY2026 vs $25M target)**
structural.threshold: <$15M FY2026 cumulative by Q3 = miss; <$10M = thesis stress

structural.current: $15-20M FY2026 target; ramping through FY2026 with full realisation Q3-Q4. Slight benefit Q1 FY2026.
structural.name: **Project CORE annual savings ($M, FY2026 ramp)**

structural.current: $27M repaid in Q1 FY2026 (on pace for $100M FY target)
structural.name: **Debt repayment pace (cumulative $M vs $100M FY2026 target)**
structural.threshold: <$60M FY cumulative by Q3 = trajectory miss

structural.current: **2 ratings** on MarketWatch (vs Benzinga's prior 1, unconfirmed whether new initiation). Confirmed coverage: Wells Fargo Hold, BofA, Barclays, Vertical Research, Mizuho per IR page (2026-04-08 fact-check). MarketBeat consensus PT $14.
structural.name: **Active analyst coverage count**
structural.source: MarketWatch; brief §3; Wells Fargo (Hajde) Apr 13-14 reaffirm Hold $12 [^16][^17][^18]
structural.threshold: New buy initiation = entry-trigger candidate per THESIS §Entry conditions; loss to 0 = discovery risk

structural.current: Investigation in progress; ~May 2026 conclusion estimate (management). Specific tariff rates not yet available.
structural.name: **South America antidumping case (Brazil SECEX Ord. 86/2025, China/Egypt/Israel imports)**
structural.source: Q1 FY2026 transcript; Global Trade Alert; SECEX Ord. 86/2025 (28 Oct 2025)
structural.threshold: Tariff imposed on Asian imports = SA volume tailwind; Investigation dismissed = structural import competition risk persists

structural.current: FY2026 guide $380-410M reaffirmed Q1 (Feb 5). Q1 EBITDA $93M (annualised ~$372M, low end of range). Bear trigger $340M for thesis stress.
structural.name: **EBITDA floor (FY2026 actuals vs $380M low end + $340M bear trigger)**
structural.threshold: <$340M FY2026 actual = bear case validated; >$410M = bull-case

structural.current: IT platform migration + control standardization expected through FY2026. Q2/Q3 FY2026 monitoring window.
structural.name: **Material weakness remediation (3 ICFR weaknesses identified)**
structural.threshold: Any new material weakness disclosed = thesis-altering; existing 3 remediated by Q4 FY2026 = positive
